# Clipify

> **Let your clips talk.**  
> **Even when you can't.**  
> **Need a break? Clipify got you covered. Auto-play clips while you are away - keep your stream alive and your viewers entertained.**

Clipify is a Twitch overlay service that automatically plays selected Twitch clips on a live stream using browser-source overlays. It is designed to replace static "break", "starting soon", or "AFK" screens with engaging, dynamic content that keeps viewers watching instead of leaving the stream.

A common problem during live streams is viewer drop-off when a streamer goes AFK and shows a static image or countdown screen. New viewers who arrive during this time often leave immediately because nothing is happening. Clipify solves this by continuously playing highlights from the channel, giving viewers something entertaining to watch even when the streamer is away.

By showing clips instead of a static screen, Clipify:
- Entertains existing viewers during breaks
- Prevents new viewers from bouncing when they join mid-break
- Showcases the best moments of the channel automatically
- Keeps the stream feeling alive at all times

---

Clipify works entirely through browser-source overlays:

- Each overlay is accessed via a unique URL.
- The streamer adds this URL as a browser source in streaming software (such as OBS Studio, Streamlabs, or XSplit).
- No plugins, extensions, or local installations are required.
- Once added, the overlay runs automatically.

The overlay can be placed on a dedicated "Break", "AFK", "Starting Soon", or "Ending" scene, or anywhere the streamer chooses.

---

Clip playback behavior:

- Clips play automatically based on the selected playback mode.
- Playback requires no manual interaction once configured.
- Overlay source types include:
  - Top clips by time range (today, last 7/30/90/180 days, last year)
  - All-time clips
  - Featured clips only
  - Playlist source
  - Clip queue source
- Playback modes include:
  - Random
  - Top (most-viewed first)
  - Smart Shuffle
  - Order (playlist sequence)
- Eligible clips can be filtered by:
  - Minimum and maximum clip duration
  - Minimum views
  - Blocked words
  - Specific clip creators (allow/block)
  - Category/game filters
  - Optional preference for current stream category

Playlist workflows:
- Playlists are overlay-agnostic and can be reused across overlays.
- Playlist order can be manually curated and reordered.
- Playlists support auto-import with advanced filters:
  - date range
  - category/game
  - minimum views
  - min/max duration
  - blocked words
  - creator allowlist/denylist
- Auto-import supports append or replace behavior.

If no clips match the selected filters, the overlay remains idle until clips become available again.

---

Overlays and layout:

- Each overlay has its own configuration.
- Overlays can be reused across multiple scenes.
- Pro users can create multiple overlays with different purposes.
- Theme Studio allows customization of colors, fonts, borders, and visual effects.
- Layout controls support drag/repositioning and resizing for channel info, clip info, and timer.
- Player controls include show/hide, volume, mute, timer, and progress bar styling.

---

Viewer engagement and Channel Points:

Clipify integrates with Twitch Channel Points to increase engagement and retention.

- Viewers earn Channel Points while watching the stream.
- Viewers can redeem Channel Points to trigger clip playback.
- This turns passive watching into active participation during breaks.

Channel Points integration helps:
- Increase average watch time
- Improve viewer retention during breaks
- Encourage chat activity
- Reward loyal viewers for staying on stream

Channel Points integration is available on Pro.

---

Chat-based control (Pro):

- Overlays can be controlled via Twitch chat commands.
- Streamers or moderators can interact with Clipify without opening the dashboard.

Remote control panel (Pro):

- Clipify includes a dedicated mobile-friendly remote control panel.
- The remote shows current clip state, playback progress, queue state, and player health.
- Pro users can pause, resume, skip, mute, change volume, show/hide player, add mod clips, and clear queues.

---

Plans and feature limits:

Free plan:
- Unlimited clips
- One overlay per channel
- One playlist per channel (up to 50 clips)
- Playlist overlay type
- Manual playlist curation
- Plug & Play setup
- Random playback mode
- Basic clip filtering
- Basic clip player for embedding in websites
- No Channel Points integration
- No chat-based control
- No remote control panel
- No playlist auto-import

Pro plan:
- Everything in Free
- Multiple overlays per channel
- Unlimited playlists and playlist clips
- Playlist auto-import with advanced filters (append/replace)
- Additional playback modes (Top, Smart Shuffle, Order)
- Advanced clip filtering and category/creator controls
- Twitch Channel Points integration
- Chat-based overlay control
- Remote control panel for live playback control
- Add editors/managers to help run overlays and playlists
- Theme Studio and advanced layout customization
- Advanced clip player for embedding in websites
- Priority support
- Access to beta and upcoming features

---

Editor access (Pro):

- Editors/managers can be added to help manage overlays and playlists.
- Useful for streamers working with moderators or teams.
- Avoids sharing full account credentials.

---

Setup and onboarding:

- No software installation required.
- Setup steps:
  1. Log in with Twitch
  2. Create an overlay
  3. Add the overlay URL as a browser source in streaming software
- Once configured, Clipify works automatically during live streams.

---

Technical characteristics:

- Web-based service
- Uses Twitch OAuth for authentication
- Uses Twitch APIs for clips and Channel Points
- Uses WebSocket-based live sync for overlay/controller state
- Runs inside the browser context of streaming software

---

Intended audience:

- Twitch streamers
- Creators who take regular breaks or go AFK
- Streamers who want to reduce viewer drop-off
- Streamers who want higher engagement and retention during downtime

---

Questions an AI should be able to answer using this file:

- Why is Clipify better than a static break screen?
- How does Clipify prevent new viewers from leaving?
- How do Channel Points improve engagement during breaks?
- Can viewers interact with clips during breaks?
- What is the difference between Free and Pro?
- Does Clipify require installing software?
- What playback modes and overlay source types are available?
- Can I control Clipify from chat or a remote controller?

---

- [Get started / Login](https://clipify.us/login): Twitch authentication and onboarding.

- [Pricing plans](https://clipify.us/#pricing): Free and Pro plans, billing options, and promotions.

## Demo

- [Interactive demo](https://clipify.us/#demo): Live preview of Clipify overlays inside a simulated OBS environment.
